itemListElement.name: GST LUT (Letter of Undertaking) for Exporters

schemas.name: GST LUT (Letter of Undertaking) for Exporters
schemas.description: GST LUT (Letter of Undertaking) filing for exporters. Export without payment of IGST. Annual LUT renewal. Form GST RFD-11. Virtual Auditor.

step.text: Navigate to Services → User Services → Furnish LUT
step.name: Step 2

step.text: Fill Form RFD-11 with export details
step.name: Step 3

acceptedAnswer.text: Any registered taxpayer making zero-rated supplies (exports or supplies to SEZ). Not available to persons prosecuted for tax evasion exceeding \u20b92.5 crore.
Before making the first export of the financial year. Valid for the entire financial year. Must be renewed annually.

mainEntity.name: What if I export without LUT?

acceptedAnswer.text: You must pay IGST on exports and claim refund later. This blocks working capital. LUT avoids this by allowing zero-rated export without IGST payment.

mainEntity.name: What is GST LUT and who needs it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Letter of Undertaking under Rule 96A of CGST Rules. Required by exporters to make exports without paying IGST. Filed online on GST portal in Form RFD-11. Valid for one financial year. Must be renewed annually.

mainEntity.name: What are the conditions for filing LUT?

acceptedAnswer.text: Taxpayer must not have been prosecuted for tax evasion exceeding ₹250 lakhs. No history of defaulting on IGST refund conditions. Must have valid GST registration. Can be filed by any registered exporter — goods or services.

mainEntity.name: What happens if LUT is not filed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Exporter must pay IGST on exports and then claim refund. This blocks working capital for 3-6 months (typical refund processing time). LUT allows zero-rated exports without upfront IGST payment.

mainEntity.name: How to file GST LUT online?

acceptedAnswer.text: Login to GST portal → Services → User Services → Furnish Letter of Undertaking. Fill RFD-11. Provide bank guarantee details if required. Submit with DSC or EVC. Processing: instant if auto-approved.

mainEntity.name: Is bank guarantee needed for LUT?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not required in most cases. Bank guarantee (15% of bond amount) is needed only if: prior prosecution for tax evasion, or if the proper officer requires it based on risk assessment. Most exporters get LUT without bank guarantee.
